title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.title: How to Make Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb — Easy Turkish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb. This easy Turkish recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb recipe
authentic Turkish Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Turkish Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb — a beloved Turkish classic

description: Kuzu Tandir is a magnificent Turkish slow-roasted lamb dish, celebrated for its incredibly tender meat that effortlessly falls off the bone. This culinary masterpiece features lamb seasoned simply and cooked patiently to achieve a rich, succulent flavor and melt-in-your-mouth texture. It's a centerpiece dish perfect for special occasions, bringing the authentic taste of Anatolian hospitality to your table.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb leg or shoulder dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep incisions all over the lamb. This helps the seasoning penetrate deeply.

instructions.title: Season the Lamb
instructions.text: In a small bowl, mix olive oil, salt, black pepper, dried oregano, and dried thyme. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the incisions for maximum flavor.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb (Optional)
instructions.text: Heat a large, heavy-bottomed oven-safe pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until nicely browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. This step adds a crucial layer of flavor and color.

instructions.title: Arrange for Roasting
instructions.text: Remove the lamb from the pot. Place the quartered onions and whole garlic cloves at the bottom of the pot, creating a bed for the lamb. Return the seared lamb to rest on top of the aromatics. Pour 2 cups of hot water or lamb broth into the pot.

instructions.title: Slow Roasting
instructions.text: Cover the pot tightly with its lid or heavy-duty aluminum foil. Transfer to a preheated oven at 160°C (325°F). Roast for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone. Baste occasionally with the pan juices, optionally adding butter for extra richness during the last hour.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the pot from the oven. Let the lamb rest, still covered, for at least 15-20 minutes before carving or shredding. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness and flavor. Serve hot with traditional Turkish rice pilaf or roasted vegetables.

tips: For the most authentic Kuzu Tandir, always use a bone-in cut of lamb. The bone contributes significantly to the flavor and moisture retention during the long cooking process.

Patience is key for Kuzu Tandir. Do not rush the cooking. The low and slow temperature is essential for breaking down the tough fibers in the lamb, resulting in its signature fall-off-the-bone tenderness.
Serve Kuzu Tandir with a side of traditional Turkish rice pilaf (Pirinç Pilavı) and a fresh green salad or a cooling cacık (cucumber yogurt dip) to perfectly complement the rich flavor of the meat.
article: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb is a beloved Turkish Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
